steps.title: Identify vertex form
steps.content: The given quadratic function $f(x)=(x-1)^{2}+7$ is in the vertex form: $y=a(x-h)^{2}+k$. Here, $(h,k)$ represents the vertex of the parabola.

steps.title: Determine minimum value
steps.content:
In this form, since $a=1>0$, the parabola opens upwards. Therefore, its vertex $(h,k)=(1,7)$ represents the lowest point on the graph.
The y-coordinate of the vertex gives us the minimum value of the function.

steps.title: Conclude
steps.content: Since $k=7$, this is also the minimum value of $f(x)$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
